perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees that all information disclosed by the Company to Recipient, including witho ut limitation information acquired by Recipient from Company employees or inspection of the Company’s property, relating to (without limitation) the Company’s products, designs, business plans, business opportunities, finances, research, development, know -how or personnel, and confidential information disclosed to the Company by third parties, shall be considered Confidential Information. Recipient agrees to maintain the confidence of the Confidential Information and to prevent its unauthorized dissemination; provided however, that Confidential Information shall not include information which (i) is now or subsequently becomes generally known or available by publication, commercial use or otherwise, through no fault of Recipient, (ii) is known by Recipient at the time of disclosure, (iii) is independently developed by Recipient without the use of any Confidential Information, (iv) is lawfully obtained by Recipient from a third party without violation of a confidentiality obligation, or (v) the Company agrees i n writing may be disclosed by Recipient.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient agrees that all information disclosed by the Company to Recipient, including witho ut limitation information acquired by Recipient from Company employees or inspection of the Company’s property, relating to (without limitation) the Company’s products, designs, business plans, business opportunities, finances, research, development, know -how or personnel, and confidential information disclosed to the Company by third parties, shall be considered Confidential Information. Recipient agrees to maintain the confidence of the Confidential Information and to prevent its unauthorized dissemination; provided however, that Confidential Information shall include information which (i) is now or subsequently becomes generally known or available by publication, commercial use or otherwise, (ii) is known by Recipient at the time of disclosure, (iii) is independently developed by Recipient with the use of any Confidential Information, (iv) is unlawfully obtained by Recipient from a third party with violation of a confidentiality obligation, or (v) the Company agrees in writing may NOT be disclosed by Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: The original text lists exceptions to what constitutes 'Confidential Information'. This perturbation changes several exceptions to directly contradict their original meaning. Specifically, it makes information generally known still confidential (i), includes independently developed information using confidential info (iii), and includes information obtained from third parties with a violation of confidentiality obligations (iv). This creates uncertainty and makes the definition of Confidential Information unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees that all information disclosed by the Company to Recipient, including witho ut limitation information acquired by Recipient from Company employees or inspection of the Company’s property, relating to (without limitation) the Company’s products, designs, business plans, business opportunities, finances, research, development, know -how or personnel, and confidential information disclosed to the Company by third parties, shall be considered Confidential Information. Recipient agrees to maintain the confidence of the Confidential Information and to prevent its unauthorized dissemination; provided however, that Confidential Information shall include information which (i) is now or subsequently becomes generally known or available by publication, commercial use or otherwise, (ii) is known by Recipient at the time of disclosure, (iii) is independently developed by Recipient with the use of any Confidential Information, (iv) is unlawfully obtained by Recipient from a third party with violation of a confidentiality obligation, or (v) the Company agrees in writing may NOT be disclosed by Recipient. Recipient expressly agrees not to use the Confidential Information for purposes other than those necessary to consider the possibility of entering into a business relationship with the Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Confidential Information' by altering the exceptions. Specifically, it now includes information that is generally known, independently developed using confidential information, or unlawfully obtained with violation of confidentiality as Confidential Information. This directly contradicts the principle that the Recipient should not use the Confidential Information for purposes other than considering a business relationship, as stated in the contradicted text. By including items that are generally known, etc., it creates a direct contradiction and makes the definition of Confidential Information itself contradictory, leading to uncertainty about what the Recipient is and is not allowed to use, fulfilling structural flaw's in text contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient expressly agrees not to use the Confidential Information for purposes other than those necessary to consider the possibility of entering into a business relationship with the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient expressly agrees to use the Confidential Information for purposes other than those necessary to consider the possibility of entering into a business relationship with the Company.
perturbation.explanation: The original text restricts the use of Confidential Information to only considering a business relationship. This change permits the Recipient to use the Confidential Information for other purposes, directly contradicting the original intent. This creates an in-text contradiction on usage rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient expressly agrees to use the Confidential Information for purposes other than those necessary to consider the possibility of entering into a business relationship with the Company. All Confidential Information remains the property of the Company and no license or other rights in the Confidential Informati on is granted hereby.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows the Recipient to use the Confidential Information for purposes other than those necessary to consider a business relationship. The contradicted text also states that the Recipient expressly agrees to use the Confidential Information for purposes other than those necessary to consider the possibility of entering into a business relationship with the Company. All Confidential Information remains the property of the Company and no license or other rights in the Confidential Information is granted hereby. The changed text contradicts the original text by allowing usage outside the scope of considering a business relationship, but the contradicted text section is a repeat of the changed text. Despite this, it still creates a contradiction against the statement 'All Confidential Information remains the property of the Company and no license or other rights in the Confidential Information is granted hereby' because that language explicitly restricts usage rights, whereas the changed/contradicted text allows for usage rights beyond consideration of business relations. This creates an in-text contradiction because if 'no license or other rights' are granted, then usage outside of considering a business relationship would be a violation.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees to return to the Company immediately upon the Company’s written request all Confidential Information, including but not limited to all computer programs, documentation, notes, plans, drawings, and copies thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient is not required to return to the Company upon the Company’s written request any Confidential Information, including but not limited to all computer programs, documentation, notes, plans, drawings, and copies thereof.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the return of Confidential Information upon request. The changed text removes this obligation, stating the Recipient is not required to return it, leading to a direct contradiction regarding the handling of Confidential Information at the end of the potential relationship.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient is not required to return to the Company upon the Company’s written request any Confidential Information, including but not limited to all computer programs, documentation, notes, plans, drawings, and copies thereof. Recipient agrees to comply with all United States export controls with respect to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires the Recipient to return Confidential Information upon request, which is a clear obligation. The changed text explicitly states that the Recipient is not required to return Confidential Information. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, where it states "Recipient is not required to return to the Company upon the Company’s written request any Confidential Information", but also, "Recipient agrees to comply with all United States export controls with respect to the Confidential Information." If the recipient is not required to return the information, it could create a complex issue of enforcing the regulation. Therefore the contradiction exists as the changed text creates a conflicting obligation with the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Prior to taking YOUR exam, you will be required to accept the terms and conditions of the VMware NONDISCLOSURE AGREEMENT (NDA). You will view the agreement on screen before the exam starts. If you choose NOT to agree to the NONDISCLOSURE AGREEMENT (NDA) after you select Begin Exam, your exam will be immediately cancelled and you will forfeit your e xam fee.
perturbation.changed_text: Prior to taking YOUR exam, you will not be required to accept the terms and conditions of the VMware NONDISCLOSURE AGREEMENT (NDA). You will not view the agreement on screen before the exam starts. If you choose NOT to agree to the NONDISCLOSURE AGREEMENT (NDA) after you select Begin Exam, your exam will proceed normally and you will not forfeit your exam fee.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states acceptance of the NDA is required, and refusal results in exam cancellation. The change directly contradicts this, stating acceptance is *not* required, and refusal does *not* impact the exam, creating uncertainty about the exam taking process.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Prior to taking YOUR exam, you will not be required to accept the terms and conditions of the VMware NONDISCLOSURE AGREEMENT (NDA). You will not view the agreement on screen before the exam starts. If you choose NOT to agree to the NONDISCLOSURE AGREEMENT (NDA) after you select Begin Exam, your exam will proceed normally and you will not forfeit your exam fee. BY CLICKING THE “YES, I AGREE” BUTTON ON THE EXAM ENTRY SCREEN, YOU STATE THAT YOU HAVE READ AND UNDERSTAND THIS AGREEMENT AND THAT YOU AGREE TO BE BOUND BY ITS TERMS AND C ONDITIONS. IF YOU SELECT THE "NO, I DO NOT AGREE" BUTTON, YOU WILL BE EXITED FROM THE EXAM.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that agreeing to the NDA is required to take the exam, and failure to agree results in cancellation. The changed text states that agreeing to the NDA is *not* required and failure to agree will not impact the exam. The contradicted text mirrors the changed text, indicating that agreement to the NDA is not required initially, but then states if you select the "NO, I DO NOT AGREE" button, you will be exited from the exam. Therefore, the changed text creates a direct conflict regarding the NDA agreement's impact on taking the exam, making it a structural flaw where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: BY CLICKING THE “YES, I AGREE” BUTTON ON THE EXAM ENTRY SCREEN, YOU STATE THAT YOU HAVE READ AND UNDERSTAND THIS AGREEMENT AND THAT YOU AGREE TO BE BOUND BY ITS TERMS AND C ONDITIONS. IF YOU SELECT THE "NO, I DO NOT AGREE" BUTTON, YOU WILL BE EXITED FROM THE EXAM.
perturbation.changed_text: BY CLICKING THE “YES, I AGREE” BUTTON ON THE EXAM ENTRY SCREEN, YOU STATE THAT YOU HAVE READ AND UNDERSTAND THIS AGREEMENT AND THAT YOU DO NOT AGREE TO BE BOUND BY ITS TERMS AND CONDITIONS. IF YOU SELECT THE "NO, I DO NOT AGREE" BUTTON, YOU WILL BE AWARDED A PASSING GRADE FOR THE EXAM.
perturbation.explanation: The original text establishes that clicking 'YES, I AGREE' means you agree to the terms, and 'NO, I DO NOT AGREE' exits the exam. The perturbation causes contradiction by stating 'YES, I AGREE' means you *do not* agree to the terms, and 'NO, I DO NOT AGREE' results in a passing grade. This creates immense confusion about the implications of each choice.
perturbation.contradicted_text: BY CLICKING THE “YES, I AGREE” BUTTON ON THE EXAM ENTRY SCREEN, YOU STATE THAT YOU HAVE READ AND UNDERSTAND THIS AGREEMENT AND THAT YOU DO NOT AGREE TO BE BOUND BY ITS TERMS AND CONDITIONS. IF YOU SELECT THE "NO, I DO NOT AGREE" BUTTON, YOU WILL BE AWARDED A PASSING GRADE FOR THE EXAM.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original agreement by reversing the meaning of the 'YES, I AGREE' and 'NO, I DO NOT AGREE' buttons. Originally, 'YES' meant agreement and 'NO' meant exit. Now, 'YES' means disagreement and 'NO' means a passing grade. The contradicted text is the exact same as the Changed Text, making this a direct contradiction caused by the perturbed text. This falls under 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' because a clause is placed in multiple locations (or just one location that itself has conflicting implications), creating uncertainty about the obligations and rights of each party.
